summary:
The principal fibers of the periodontal ligament consist mainly of collagen, providing essential support against mechanical stresses. Collagen dominates as the primary component without significant contradictory information suggesting other types play similar major roles within them.
The principal fibers of the periodontal ligament primarily comprise collagen, offering crucial stabilization through their strength; existing research does not indicate an equally prominent presence from different fiber types alongside it. \boxed{\text{C}} To summarize:

- Principal fibers of the PDL are mostly composed of collagen.
- These fibers provide vital structural support by resisting mechanical forces.
